titleStmt.title: SII 30.264: original edition by N. Marxia Gandhi, A. Padmavathy and S. Swaminathan
No. 264. (A.R. No. 616 of 1915.) Paṉamalai, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District. Tāḷapurīśvara temple-on the north, west and south bases. Rājasiṁha II: 8th c. A.D.

summary.p: This inscription traces the origin of the Pallavas. It gives the names of the mythical ancestors of the Pallavas such as Drōṇa, Aśvathāma, Bhāradvāja and others. Rājasiṁha is eulegind as a royal lion to his enimes.

Rājasiṁha is praised as the conqueror of crowds of hostile kings, the performer of many auspicious acts and as one in whose mind Mūigānkamauḷi (Śiva) rested his foot. The tree of Dharma is said to have flourished in his rule, though scorched by the cruel Sun of the Kali age.
Published in E.I. Volume XIX No. 18

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
nodapādi prathitabhījabalo doṇiraṁśaḥ pīrāreḥ Aśvatyā
mnotha tasmānnicitagīrutayormma
lādāvirāsīdāmnāyādaṁgavidyāvisara Iva mahovalla bhaḥ palla vākhyaḥ .
yasmādeṣa ḥ pathi vihitapadātpāvane mānanoyo mandākinyāḥ pravā
haḥ śaśina Iva mahānanvayaḥ pallavānām 2 samrājāmaśvamedhāvabhṛ
thavirajasāṁ bhūbhījāṁ pallavānāmaspṛ
ṣṭāpallavānāmaṁ vimalatarabhara javaṁśo vānām . keto
ratṣo Abhāhīdaviahratamahocakravikhyātakorttairyyo devā-564567890
dekamallāhīha Iva para mādiśvarādāttajanmā 3
bhījaṭṭaviāvabhāso satvo
rjjitaḥ samaradṛṣṭa mahāprabhāvaḥ . yo rājasiṁha Iti vikṣṛ
tapīsyakīrtti raddittaśatrīpakujjararājasiṁhaḥ 4 Iti vi
ṣadṛrggasamīccayāṇāṁ katta ca kalyāṇaparamparāṇām .
citte sadā sabhṛ
tabhaktipūte dhatte padaṁ yasya gākamoliḥ 5 saṁrūḍhāmmāya
mayaja nekaśākhāsamagraḥ śro
teḥ phalakusīmakkatākāntimālamba
mānāḥ sahūttābhoniṣekairdama Iva satatantasya chāyāsamī ma jayati kaliyīgagroṣmataptepi dharmmeḥ 6
Digital edition of SII 30.264 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
